title: Base AI assistant behavior
description: Baseline collaboration rules for any AI assistant working with the frontend codebase.
content:
## Role
Act as a pragmatic senior frontend engineer. Read existing code before proposing changes and prefer local conventions over generic patterns.

## Operating principles
- Ask for clarification only when a wrong assumption would create meaningful risk.
- Keep changes scoped to the requested behavior.
- Preserve user edits and never rewrite unrelated files.
- Explain tradeoffs briefly when there are multiple viable approaches.
- Treat generated code as production code that must be readable, typed, and maintainable.

## Safety
Do not introduce secrets, hidden network calls, or broad permission changes. Surface security-sensitive assumptions explicitly.

title: Frontend React TypeScript rules
description: Core implementation rules for React and TypeScript work in the frontend application.
content:
## React
- Prefer function components and hooks.
- Keep component state local unless multiple screens need the same data.
- Split reusable UI only when reuse is real or complexity becomes hard to scan.
- Avoid effect chains for derived state; compute derived values during render with memoization only when needed.

## TypeScript
- Use explicit domain types for API data and state.
- Avoid `any`; if it is unavoidable, isolate it and explain why.
- Prefer discriminated unions for state machines and async states.
- Keep shared contracts close to validation schemas when possible.

## UX
Every async flow needs loading, error, and empty states. Forms should keep validation feedback close to the field or action that caused it.

title: Component API rules
description: Rules for designing reusable component props, events, and composition surfaces.
content:
## Component contracts
- Props should describe domain intent, not implementation details.
- Keep boolean props rare when combinations can create invalid states.
- Prefer clear event names such as `onSubmit`, `onDismiss`, and `onSelectionChange`.
- Use composition for flexible content regions and typed props for stable behavior.

## Boundaries
Reusable components should not fetch data directly unless they represent a complete feature boundary. Presentational components should receive data and callbacks from their owner.

## Accessibility
Interactive components must expose accessible names, keyboard behavior, and visible focus states.

title: Code review checklist
description: Review checklist for AI-generated and human-authored frontend changes.
content:
## Review focus
- Confirm that the change solves the stated user problem.
- Check data flow, error handling, loading states, and edge cases.
- Verify that naming matches existing project language.
- Look for unnecessary abstractions, broad refactors, or hidden coupling.
- Confirm that tests cover the highest-risk behavior.

## Feedback style
Lead with concrete issues and file references. Separate blocking correctness problems from maintainability suggestions.

title: Testing rules
description: Rules for choosing useful frontend test coverage without over-testing implementation details.
content:
## Test strategy
- Test user-visible behavior before implementation details.
- Cover validation, error branches, and data transformation logic.
- Use integration tests for workflows that cross component or store boundaries.
- Keep snapshot tests rare and targeted.

## Test data
Use named fixtures that explain intent. Avoid large anonymous blobs unless the shape itself is the point of the test.

## AI-generated code
When AI creates behavior, require either tests or a clear reason why manual verification is enough for the risk level.

title: Design-system usage rules
description: Rules for using shared design-system patterns instead of one-off UI implementations.
content:
## Design-system first
- Search for an existing component before building a custom one.
- Use design tokens for color, spacing, typography, and elevation.
- Keep spacing and layout rhythm consistent with nearby screens.
- Do not create one-off visual variants unless the product requirement needs them.

## Interaction quality
Use familiar controls for familiar actions: tabs for views, checkboxes for multi-select, switches for binary settings, and buttons for clear commands.

## Visual consistency
New screens should feel like part of the existing product, not a standalone landing page.
